input: Schedule a meeting with the design team next Tuesday at 2pm for 1 hour in CET timezone and send them an email reminder about reviewing the new mockups.
expected_output:
The reminder email has been sent to design-team@example.com.

I couldn’t create the calendar event yet because I need the attendees’ email addresses for the design team.

Planned event:
- Title: Design team meeting
- Time: Next Tuesday at 2:00 PM CET
- Duration: 1 hour

If you send me the team’s email addresses, I can finish scheduling it.

input: Block 90 minutes this Friday morning for deep work—no attendees—and email my manager that I’ll be offline during that window unless it’s urgent.
expected_output:
I need one quick detail before I can finish both actions: what time zone should I use for “this Friday morning”?

Optional: if you have a preferred start time, tell me that too. Otherwise I can pick a 90-minute slot in the morning.

Once you confirm, I’ll:
1. block the deep work time on your calendar, and
2. email your manager that you’ll be offline during that window unless it’s urgent.

input: Find a time Thursday afternoon for me, Priya (priya@acme.co), and Luis (luis@acme.co) for a 45-minute architecture review, book it, then email both of them the agenda: API versioning, rollout risks, and open questions.
expected_output:
Done — I booked the architecture review for Thursday, April 30, 2026, from 2:00–2:45 PM, and emailed Priya and Luis the agenda:

- API versioning
- Rollout risks
- Open questions

input: Send the recruiting team a thank-you email for yesterday’s onsite loop and mention we’ll share consolidated feedback by end of week.
expected_output:
I need the recruiting team’s email address(es) to send it.

Please share the recipient email(s), and I’ll send a thank-you note saying thanks for yesterday’s onsite loop and that consolidated feedback will be shared by end of week.

input: Move our weekly standup to Wednesdays at 9:30am for 30 minutes through the end of the month—same video link—and ping the team in email so nobody misses it.
expected_output:
I need one quick detail before I can do both actions:

- What time zone should I use for Wednesday at 9:30am?
- Also, what’s the exact current standup event title or attendee list so I can identify the right series and keep the same video link?

Once you send that, I can:
1. update/create the Wednesday 9:30–10:00am standups through the end of the month, and
2. email the team so they don’t miss it.

input: Reserve next Thursday 4pm–5pm for interview debrief with the panel (same people as last time—use their emails from the last debrief invite) and don’t send any email; I’ll Slack them myself.
expected_output:
I can schedule the time, but I need the panelists’ email addresses since I can’t look up attendees from the last debrief invite.

Also, I can’t guarantee suppressing calendar email notifications with this tool.

Send me the panel emails and I’ll create:
- Interview debrief with the panel
- Next Thursday, 4:00–5:00 PM

input: Schedule a 1-hour product roadmap session next Monday at 11am with product@ourco.com and eng-leads@ourco.com in Conference Room B.
expected_output:
I can schedule it, but I need the exact date for “next Monday” and your timezone.

Planned details:
- Product roadmap session
- 1 hour
- 11:00 AM
- Attendees: product@ourco.com, eng-leads@ourco.com
- Location: Conference Room B

Reply with the date/timezone, for example: “May 5, 11am PT.”

input: Email legal@ourco.com with subject "NDA for Contoso pilot" and say we’re ready for their redlines; attach nothing for now. No calendar changes.
expected_output: Done — the email was sent to legal@ourco.com with subject “NDA for Contoso pilot.” No calendar changes were made.

input: Book a 20-minute sync with Alex (alex@partner.io) tomorrow late afternoon if there’s a slot, and send them a quick email with our latest pricing one-pager summary in the body—no file attach.
expected_output: Done — I booked the sync with Alex for tomorrow, April 29, 2026, from 4:00 PM to 4:20 PM, and sent them a brief email with the pricing summary in the body, no attachment.

input: On March 12, hold a 2-hour workshop from 1pm to 3pm for the customer success team only, then email CS-all@ourco.com the pre-read link https://docs.example.com/cs-workshop and ask them to skim it before the session.
expected_output: Done — I scheduled the 2-hour Customer Success Workshop for March 12 from 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM, and sent the pre-read email to CS-all@ourco.com.

input: Check when everyone on the exec@ourco.com list is free next Wednesday for a 60-minute QBR dry run, pick the best option, put it on the calendar, and email them the deck outline: metrics, risks, asks.
expected_output:
I need the actual attendee email addresses behind `exec@ourco.com` to check calendars and book the meeting.

Please send the list of email addresses for that group, and I’ll:
1. find the best 60-minute slot next Wednesday,
2. put it on the calendar, and
3. email them the deck outline: metrics, risks, asks.

input: Send ops@ourco.com a heads-up that the vendor maintenance window slipped to Sunday 2am–6am UTC and they should pause automated deploys during that window.
expected_output: Done — I sent the email to ops@ourco.com.
